text: Jensen Model C-V8 Instruction Book cover
Handbook of instructions for the Jensen CV-8. Published by Jensen Motors Limited West Bromwich England.

Contents index including instruments and controls, specifications, starting and running, lubrication, electrical equipment, brakes, fuel system, carburetter, tyres, front suspension, cooling system, rear axle, drive belt adjustment.
Instruments and controls dashboard diagram and numbered list of dashboard switches gauges warning lights heater controls ignition lighting gear selector and auxiliary controls.
General dimensions and data quick reference engine bore stroke compression ratio dimensions weights turning circle fuel tank and other specifications.

Quick reference data including firing order 1-8-4-3-6-5-7-2, spark plug gap .035 inch, ignition timing 10 degrees before TDC at 500 rpm, tyres Dunlop 670 x 15 road speed, tyre pressure 24 psi all round, sump capacity 7 pints Imperial 8 pints US 3.6 litres.

Starting up and general running hints. Running in, avoid sustained high speeds, watch temperature and oil pressure. Starting car engine with automatic transmission neutral, handbrake on, accelerator one third travel, release key when engine fires.
Gearbox operation automatic transmission selector, push starting and towing instructions. Automatic carburetter control may cause fast idle when cold.
Optional manual transmission and overdrive operation. Overdrive controlled by switch and accelerator kick-down arrangements.
Lubrication and maintenance schedule first 1000 miles and recurring service intervals. Check fluids, belt, electrics, tyre pressures, lights, brakes, controls.
Lubrication chart diagram showing underside service and grease points including king pins, propeller shaft, steering idler, steering rack and rear axle items.

Electrical equipment. Lucas equipment except ignition charging and starter systems which are Autolite. Battery is 12 volt Lucas and positive earth. Fuses are 35 amp main and auxiliary, fuse box forward and below right hand side of fascia.

Electrical lamps, headlamps, screenwiper, screen cleaner, map light, gear quadrant light. Brakes and brake servo: Dunlop hydraulic disc brakes all wheels, vacuum servo, separate handbrake, check brake fluid every 2000 miles, do not drill frame tubes used as vacuum reservoirs.
Fuel system and Carter carburetter diagrams. Fuel tank, lines, filters, mechanical pump and carburetter component identification.
Idle speed adjustment for Carter carburetter. Warm engine, choke valve fully open, fast idle screw clear of cam, set idle speed about 500 rpm, adjust mixture screws for highest rpm then recheck idle.
Fast idle speed adjustment and throttle linkage diagrams. Fast idle adjusting screw, choke valve, cam, connector rod, throttle linkage factory relationship.
Accelerator pedal adjustment diagram. Care of tyres, tyre pressures and tyre life factors.
Tyre and wheel balance, factors affecting tyre life, spare wheel removal and front suspension.
Alignment of front wheels and adjusting front wheel bearings. Toe-in measured at wheel height should be 1/8 inch. Front hub and bearing section diagram.

Electric cooling fans, heating and ventilation, cooling system, topping up, rear axle Power-Lok limited slip differential, drive belt adjustment. Do not remove header tank cap when hot. Check coolant weekly cold and top up to one inch below header tank top.
